resources.string: A focused file manager for browsing storage, reviewing recent files, and keeping everyday file work under control.
An advanced, powerful, fast, and smooth Android file manager built with Kotlin and Material Design 3.
Seamlessly manage Internal Storage, SD Cards, and USB OTG devices
Multi-select files for copy, cut, move, or permanent delete
Jump into images, videos, audio, documents, archives, and APKs
Safely remove files with metadata-aware restoration
Arcile is a personal project built with a privacy-first mindset.
Arcile does not declare the INTERNET permission, your files and activity data cannot leave your device.
The application contains zero advertisements and zero third-party tracking SDKs.
All file operations are executed locally on your device; no data is transmitted externally.
Arcile\'s source code is publicly available for inspection on GitHub.
Arcile keeps the common paths close while still respecting Android storage limits.
Open internal storage, SD cards, OTG drives, folders, archives, and recent files from one place.
Jump into images, videos, audio, documents, archives, and APKs without hunting through folders.
Supported permanent storage can use Arcile trash so accidental deletes are easier to recover.
Android/data and Android/obb open through the system Files app when Android blocks direct access.
Pick a theme and accent now, or leave the defaults and change them later in Settings.
Personalize your layout, grant storage access, and restore backups if you have them.
Required to view, organize, and manage files on your device.
You can now view and manage your files.
Arcile can read and write user-visible files on internal storage, SD cards, and supported USB drives.
Android still blocks other apps\' private Android/data folders and other restricted areas.
Android will open Settings to grant this permission, then Arcile checks it when you return.
Needed to show progress when copying, moving, or archiving files in the background.
Arcile will show background file operation progress.
This Android version does not require a separate notification permission.
Storage permission is required to continue
Some file-management features may be limited on older Android versions. Arcile will show the best supported path for this device.
Arcile is set up and ready to browse your files.
Arcile needs all-files access before it can browse and manage files. Re-enable access in Android Settings to continue.
Your files stay on this device. Arcile uses this permission only for file manager actions you start.
Arcile could not render this image on this device.
